title: Multi-tenant data model + AES-256-GCM at-rest encryption (Prisma/Postgres/pgvector)
context: All constituent and office data must be strictly tenant-scoped and encrypted at rest before any feature stores real data. The data model uses Prisma + PostgreSQL + pgvector. Sensitive fields are encrypted with AES-256-GCM; encryption keys are never committed. The constituent schema must have no political/affiliation/profiling fields by design. This task establishes the tenant boundary and encryption baseline that all subsequent data tasks (006, 007, 008, etc.) build on.
objective: Implement the multi-tenant Prisma schema with per-row office/official tenant scoping, AES-256-GCM at-rest encryption for sensitive fields, and cross-tenant isolation tests -- so all subsequent code tasks have a secure, tested data foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: Every Prisma model row is scoped to an office/official tenant; cross-tenant isolation test passes (a query scoped to tenant A cannot return tenant B rows)
Sensitive content fields are encrypted at rest with AES-256-GCM; encryption key is loaded from environment, never committed
Constituent schema has no political/affiliation/profiling fields
pgvector extension configured; vector field present on models that will use embeddings (Duties, Sources, Notes)
Prisma migrations are clean and reproducible
CI runs the cross-tenant isolation tests on every PR
output: A merged PR with the multi-tenant Prisma schema, AES-256-GCM encryption helpers, pgvector configuration, and a passing cross-tenant isolation test suite.
